title: tilia platyphyllos subsp. cordifolia
intro: Tilia platyphyllos subsp. cordifolia, also known as Large-leafed Lime or Large-leaved Linden, is a subspecies of the Tilia platyphyllos tree, native to Eastern Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Tilia platyphyllos subsp. cordifolia include relief from stress and anxiety, improved sleep quality, and a reduction in fever and pain, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Tilia platyphyllos subsp. cordifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to soothe skin irritations.
